page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg6.20xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 80 dedicated physical cores using the Intel Xeon 8269CY processor, operating at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. A key architectural characteristic is its single-thread-per-core configuration, providing 80 vCPUs without hyperthreading. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 4.8 GB of RAM per core.

This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external network-attached volumes. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps. The lack of local storage and GPU accelerators represents a clear tradeoff, positioning this instance away from data-intensive local caching or deep learning workloads, and focusing its resource profile entirely on CPU-bound execution.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.hfg6.20xlarge offers high compute density by allocating dedicated physical cores rather than shared virtual threads. This makes it highly efficient for workloads requiring predictable, sustained CPU performance without the latency spikes associated with hyperthreading. Suitable workloads include high-performance computing, compute-heavy batch processing, financial modeling, and CPU-bound application servers.

The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-effective for workloads requiring predictable, high-frequency CPU performance without hyperthreading overhead. It is suited for compute-heavy batch processing, high-performance computing, and CPU-bound application servers.
